prerequisites: An Airbyte account, either Airbyte Cloud or a Self-Managed (self-hosted) instance, whose data you want to read via the Airbyte API.
An application created in your Airbyte user settings, which provides the `client_id` and `client_secret` used for authentication.
For a Self-Managed instance, the base URL of your Airbyte deployment to supply as the optional `host` config field. Leave `host` empty to use Airbyte Cloud (api.airbyte.com).
A `start_date` value marking the earliest point from which the source should begin reading data.

authMethods.name: Client credentials (client_id + client_secret)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates to the Airbyte API using OAuth2 client-credentials. Supply the `client_id` and the secret `client_secret` generated for an application in your Airbyte user settings. The connector exchanges them for a short-lived bearer access token by POSTing client_id, client_secret, and grant-type=client_credentials to the applications token endpoint (https://api.airbyte.com/v1/applications/token for Cloud, or <host>/api/public/v1/applications/token for Self-Managed). Access tokens expire after 15 minutes and are refreshed automatically.

setupSteps.title: Create an application to get your client credentials
setupSteps.body: In the Airbyte UI, click your name, then go to User settings and open the Applications page. Select Create an application, give it a descriptive name, and submit. Reveal and copy the generated client secret. This produces the `client_id` and `client_secret` credentials the connector requires.

setupSteps.title: Provide client_id and client_secret
setupSteps.body: Enter the `client_id` and the secret `client_secret` from your Airbyte application into the connector configuration. These are exchanged for an access token automatically; no manual token handling is needed.

setupSteps.title: Set the host (Self-Managed only)
setupSteps.body: If you are reading from a Self-Managed (self-hosted) Airbyte instance, set the optional `host` field to your deployment's base URL so the connector targets <host>/api/public/v1/applications/token. Leave `host` empty to use Airbyte Cloud, which uses https://api.airbyte.com.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Provide the required `start_date` to define the earliest point from which the connector begins reading data.
